ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christie Fraser for Helen McCloskey (Petitioner). Bruce Feder for John Jameson (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it makes sense to keep the matter here, that it is not inlcined to ask for change of venue, and expressed concern about fees for preparation of the accounting and the tax returns being extremely high. Ms. Fraser explained that some of those fees are reimbursement for payments of estimated individual taxes for Mr. Jameson, stated that she filed a supplement and that she can not comment on why the taxes are so high. Mr. Feder also stated that he did not know why the taxes were high. The Court suggested using a Probate Paraglegal for preparation of the accounting and stated that both Counsel need to confer about the accountant fees. Ms. Fraser aslo addressed other issues raised in the Probate Examiner's notes (flat screen television, redocorating the room, etc.). Mr. Feder raised the issue of retention of a Case Manager, stated that he is not sure there is much more that can be done but that they should at the least have an assessment done. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
